title: The Wolves of Mercy Falls producer Mercy Falls BC Inc. obtains CCAA protection to seek completion of film
content:
## The Wolves of Mercy Falls producer Mercy Falls BC Inc. obtains CCAA protection to seek completion of film
On May 16, 2025, Mercy Falls BC Inc. obtained Companies Creditors Arrangement Act (the "CCAA") protection. McCarthy Tétrault is counsel to the Monitors, MNP Inc. (the "Monitor"). Over the course of the CCAA proceedings, the Monitor has actively overseen the debtor’s restructuring efforts, including the negotiation of interim financing arrangements and coordination of litigation strategy within the CCAA framework. The Monitor has facilitated the development of a procedural schedule for the adjudication of contractual disputes with Mercy Falls Films, LLC, ensuring that litigation proceeds efficiently within the CCAA process. Mercy Falls BC Inc. initiated the CCAA proceeding in response to the alleged failure of its U.S. production partner, Mercy Falls Films LLC, to provide promised funding. The CCAA proceeding was initiated pursuant to litigation against Mercy Falls Films for the outstanding amounts. The litigation remains ongoing.
McCarthy Tétrault is assisting the Monitor with a team led by Lance Williams and Ashley Bowron (Bankruptcy and Restructuring).
- Lance Williams Partner People.Offices.Plural Vancouver, Toronto
